mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong and healthy labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate profitability. This positive economic environment typically translates into upward pressure on equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, low unemployment correlates with economic expansion.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of periods when UNRATE is below its 10-year average, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows a significant and sustained acceleration, it signals an overheating economy or supply-side pressures. This typically prompts the central bank to adopt a more hawkish stance, increasing interest rates (FEDFUNDS), which raises the cost of capital and negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Central banks typically respond to high inflation with rate hikes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent strength in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer confidence and investment activity. This combination suggests a robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3.0% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer and housing activity are key drivers of economic growth.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST show strong annualized growth, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of aggressive monetary tightening, characterized by rapid increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), the rising cost of capital and reduced liquidity negatively impact corporate profitability, investment, and investor sentiment, leading to lower S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates increase borrowing costs for companies.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a FEDFUNDS increase of more than 0.5% over three months within a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic activity. This typically precedes or accompanies a recession, leading to a decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 2.0% annualized over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) reflects an expanding economy with increasing aggregate demand. This robust economic backdrop provides a strong fundamental basis for corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary measure of economic health.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of low unemployment, a combination of accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) and persistent consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating economy. This scenario forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy aggressively, negatively impacting equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2.5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Tight labor markets combined with inflation often lead to Fed tightening.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods when all trigger conditions are met within a low unemployment regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a significant deterioration in the labor market and often precedes or coincides with an economic recession. This leads to a decline in consumer confidence and corporate earnings, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Rapid increases in unemployment are strong recessionary signals.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.
